workers/hoox.description: Gateway worker — the only public-facing entry point. Receives TradingView webhooks, validates API keys, enforces WAF rules, manages sessions via KV, deduplicates signals via Durable Objects, and routes to trade-worker via Queue.
workers/hoox.llmContext: Start here for signal ingestion flow. hoox is the ONLY public-facing worker (POST /webhook, GET /health). Exports IdempotencyStore Durable Object class for signal deduplication. Uses shared middleware: createRateLimiter (KV-backed, session-based, 10 trades/min), withRequestLog for structured logging, validateJson with Zod schemas. Session management via httpOnly cookies stored in SESSIONS_KV (24hr expiry). Security: X-Content-Type-Options, CSP, HSTS headers, kill_switch check in CONFIG_KV, TradingView IP allowlist check, timingSafeEqual for API key comparison. Produces TradeQueueMessage to TRADE_QUEUE for async processing by trade-worker. References shared types WebhookPayloadSchema, StandardResponse. Key internal functions: checkRateLimit (session-based), checkIdempotency (via DO), sendTradeToQueue, processTrade, processNotification. Rate limiter: window=60s, max=10, prefix='session'. Security headers: 7 headers including CSP default-src 'self'.

workers/trade-worker.description: Multi-exchange execution worker. Consumes signals from TRADE_QUEUE, routes to Binance/MEXC/Bybit via ExchangeRouter, persists trades to D1, sends notifications via telegram-worker, and tracks analytics.
workers/trade-worker.llmContext: Core trade execution engine. Consumes from TRADE_QUEUE async (queue handler), also accepts direct calls via POST /webhook (service binding from hoox/email-worker), POST /process (legacy internal endpoint), GET/POST /api/signals (D1 signal CRUD), GET /report (R2 report download). Queue handler implements retry with exponential backoff: max 5 retries, delays=[0s, 30s, 1m, 5m, 15m], moves to DLQ after exhaustion. Failed trades logged to D1 system_logs table. Key exports: executeTrade (validates API creds, routes to exchange clients), DbLogger (tracks request/response pairs), MexcClient/BinanceClient/BybitClient (exchange-specific implementations), sendTradeNotification (via telegram-worker service binding). On success: queues R2 report save via saveReportToR2 (ctx.waitUntil). Analytics tracked via trackAnalytics to analytics-worker for every /webhook and /process call (latency, success). Uses requireInternalAuth middleware with INTERNAL_KEY_BINDING for service-to-service auth. Uses factories pattern with createDbLogger for testability (vi.spyOn injection point). Schema: WebhookPayload (exchange, action, symbol, quantity, price, leverage).

workers/agent-worker.description: AI risk manager. Runs on a 5-minute cron to monitor open positions, assess portfolio risk using Workers AI, and autonomously adjust positions via trade-worker or alert via telegram-worker.
workers/agent-worker.llmContext: Autonomous AI agent with multi-provider model routing. Runs on */5 * * * * cron executing runHousekeeping (reads positions from D1, checks trailing stops) and processRoutine (AI risk assessment via ProviderManager). Scheduled handler also triggered via POST /agent/housekeeping endpoint (service binding target from dashboard). REST endpoints (all require internal auth except /agent/health): POST /agent/housekeeping (trigger housekeeping), POST /agent/risk-override (set trailingStopPercent in CONFIG_KV), GET /agent/status (provider config + active trailing stops), GET/POST /agent/config (load/update AgentConfig), POST /agent/test-model (test specific provider/model), POST /agent/chat (generic AI chat with system prompt + temperature + maxTokens), POST /agent/embedding (generate text embeddings), GET /agent/models (list all available models), GET /agent/health (provider health status). ProviderManager supports fallback chain: workers-ai → openai → anthropic → google. Manages trailing stops via CONFIG_KV keys prefixed 'trade:watermark:'. Exported for testing: requireInternalAuth, checkInternalAuth, processRoutine. Key types: AIRequest (messages, model, temperature, maxTokens), AgentConfig (defaultProvider, fallbackChain, modelMap), ProviderName union. Uses createInternalAuthMiddleware for auth. Key logic files: ./logic/trade (fetchMarkPrice, sendCloseOrder), ./logic/housekeeping, ./logic/routine.

workers/d1-worker.description: Centralized D1 database access layer. All database reads/writes go through this worker to ensure consistent schema, connection pooling, and audit logging via analytics-worker.
workers/d1-worker.llmContext: Data access layer — single point of contact for ALL D1 operations. Uses D1Database from @cloudflare/workers-types with native binding DB. Table allowlist: trade_signals, trades, positions, balances, system_logs, trade_requests, trade_responses — any query referencing tables outside this allowlist is rejected (403 forbidden). Forbidden SQL keywords blocked: DROP, PRAGMA, ALTER, TRUNCATE, VACUUM, ATTACH, DETACH. Query types allowed: SELECT (returns results[]), INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/REPLACE (returns lastRowId + changes). Natively supports DB.batch() for atomic multi-statement execution. Endpoints: POST /query (single SQL with params), POST /batch (array of statements, atomic), GET/POST /api/settings (read/write config via CONFIG_KV with prefixes global:, webhook:, trade:, agent:), GET /api/balances (latest balance snapshots per exchange+asset, includes aggregated totalBalance), GET /api/positions (open positions only, ordered by updated_at DESC), GET /api/logs (last 50 system_logs entries), GET /health (validates DB connectivity with SELECT 1). All endpoints except health require internal auth via createInternalAuthMiddleware. CORS headers added to all responses (incl OPTIONS preflight). Analytics tracked via trackAnalytics for /query and /batch endpoints. CONFIG_KV used for settings storage, not DB. Uses AnalyticsEnv for Analytics Engine tracking.

workers/telegram-worker.description: Notification worker. Sends trade alerts, risk warnings, and system notifications via Telegram Bot API. Uses Workers AI for message summarization. Receives calls from multiple workers via service bindings.
workers/telegram-worker.llmContext: Notification hub — called by 6 workers (hoox, trade-worker, agent-worker, report-worker, web3-wallet-worker, email-worker) via service bindings. Endpoints: POST /process (legacy internal — parses ProcessRequestBody<NotificationPayload>, requires internal auth, calls sendTelegramNotification), POST /webhook (new service binding target — calls handleWebhookRequest). Also exports helper functions for testing: generateEmbeddings, insertEmbeddings, queryEmbeddings (RAG pipeline using Vectorize index), handleGetLatestTradeSignalR2 (reads from UPLOADS_BUCKET). On every request, logs KV timestamp via logKvTimestamp from @jango-blockchained/hoox-shared/kvUtils. NotificationPayload structure: { message: string, chatId?: string } — if chatId omitted, uses TG_CHAT_ID_BINDING secret. Uses Workers AI for message summarization before sending. Vectorize index (my-rag-index) for context retrieval. R2 user-uploads bucket for storing shared media/files.

workers/web3-wallet-worker.description: DeFi and on-chain execution worker. Interacts with blockchain protocols for wallet operations, DeFi strategies, and on-chain trade execution. Sends notifications via telegram-worker.
workers/web3-wallet-worker.llmContext: On-chain execution using ethers.js library. Endpoints: GET / (wallet initialization — reads private key from WALLET_PK_SECRET or mnemonic from WALLET_MNEMONIC_SECRET via Cloudflare Secrets Store bindings, creates ethers.Wallet or HDNodeWallet, returns wallet address), GET /health. Validates secret format: private key must match ^0x?[0-9a-fA-F]{64}$, mnemonic must have 12+ words. On successful init: sends notification via TELEGRAM_SERVICE service binding (fire-and-forget via ctx.waitUntil, calls /webhook endpoint with wallet address). Tracks analytics via trackAnalytics (endpoint: '/'). Uses createInternalAuthMiddleware for auth. Key ethers imports: ethers (v6), HDNodeWallet from mnemonic, Wallet from private key. Secrets not stored in env vars but fetched from Cloudflare Secrets Store bindings at runtime.

workers/email-worker.description: Email signal scanner. Receives trading signals via Mailgun webhook or direct JSON POST, parses them into trade signals, and forwards to trade-worker for execution.
workers/email-worker.llmContext: Email/JSON signal ingestion. Two ingress paths: (1) POST /webhook — Mailgun webhook with HMAC-SHA256 signature validation (header fields: Mailgun-Signature, Mailgun-Timestamp, Mailgun-Token, verified with MAILGUN_API_KEY via crypto.subtle), parses formData body-plain or stripped-text; (2) POST /email-signal — direct JSON POST (requires internal auth), accepts { text, body } or full JSON object. Both paths process through parseEmailSignal which tries JSON parse first, falls back to plaintext regex extraction using configurable patterns from CONFIG_KV: coinPattern (default: BTC|ETH|SOL), actionPattern (default: buy|sell|long|short), quantityMultiplier. Extracted fields: exchange (binance/mexc/bybit normalization), action (buy/sell normalization), symbol (uppercase, stripped), quantity (default 100 * multiplier), price, leverage. Forwards parsed signal to trade-worker via TRADE_SERVICE service binding (POST /webhook with X-Internal-Auth-Key and X-Source: email-worker headers). Tracks analytics via trackAnalytics on both success and failure. Scheduled handler exists but IMAP scanning is DISABLED in edge runtime (logs info message). Uses Mailgun webhook as primary ingestion path. Key types: EmailSignal { exchange, action, symbol, quantity, price?, leverage? }, SignalPatterns (coinPattern, actionPattern, quantityMultiplier). Key functions: handleMailgunWebhook (HMAC validation), handleDirectJson, processEmail, parseEmailSignal, extractFromPlaintext, extractField, loadSignalPatterns.
entryPoints: scheduled (DISABLED — IMAP not available in edge)

workers/analytics-worker.description: Analytics engine. Receives events from 6 workers via service bindings and writes to Cloudflare Analytics Engine (hoox-analytics dataset). Central observability hub.
workers/analytics-worker.llmContext: Central observability hub — fan-in analytics collector for all system workers. Uses Cloudflare Analytics Engine (ANALYTICS_ENGINE binding, dataset: hoox-analytics) for time-series metric storage. Endpoints (all POST, all require internal auth via Zod schema validation): /track/trade (TradeBodySchema: { payload: {exchange, symbol, action, quantity}, result: {success}, latencyMs }), /track/api-call (ApiCallBodySchema: { worker, endpoint, latencyMs, success } — most commonly used, called by 6 workers), /track/worker-perf (WorkerPerfBodySchema: { data: {worker, requests, errors, duration} }), /track/signal (SignalBodySchema: { data: {source, type, symbol, confidence} }), /track/notification (NotificationBodySchema: { data: {type, target, success} }), GET /health. All schemas use Zod with .strict() to reject unknown fields. Key exported functions: writeDataPoint (low-level Analytics Engine write), trackTrade, trackApiCall, trackWorkerPerf, trackSignal, trackNotification (each builds DataPoint via buildDataPoint helper with specific blobs/doubles/indexes). Query methods for dashboard/reporting: getTradeMetrics, getTradesByExchange, getTradeSuccessRate, getWorkerPerformance, getApiCallStats, getSignalOutcomes — each builds SQL via buildQuery helper and executes via Cloudflare SQL API (requires CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN + CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID). Key types: DataPoint (blobs, doubles, indexes), TradePayload, TradeResult, WorkerPerfData, SignalData, NotificationData — all in ./types.

workers/report-worker.description: PDF report generator. Runs on cron (06:00 and 18:00 UTC) to generate trading reports using Browser Rendering, stores in R2, and sends via telegram-worker.
workers/report-worker.llmContext: Automated portfolio report generator. Cron: 0 6,18 * * * (twice daily at 06:00/18:00 UTC). Scheduled handler calls generateAndStoreReport. Also triggerable via GET /report (requires internal auth, returns 202 Accepted, runs via ctx.waitUntil). Report generation flow: (1) fetchPortfolioSummary — calls d1-worker via D1_SERVICE binding (GET /api/balances + GET /api/positions, requires X-Internal-Auth-Key), aggregates totalValue, dailyPnL, totalPnL, openPositions, winRate, topAsset; (2) buildReportHtml — renders responsive HTML template with grid cards, conditional positive/negative P&L styling, A4-formatted with inline CSS; (3) generatePdf — calls Cloudflare Browser Rendering REST API (POST https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/{account}/browser-rendering/pdf with html + options { format: A4, printBackground: true, margin }), requires CF_API_TOKEN_BINDING; (4) stores PDF in REPORTS_BUCKET R2 (key: reports/daily-{timestamp}.pdf, contentType: application/pdf); (5) sendNotification — calls telegram-worker via TELEGRAM_SERVICE binding (POST /process with markdown message including signed report URL). Exported for testing: generatePdf, sendNotification, fetchPortfolioSummary, buildReportHtml, generateAndStoreReport. Uses @cloudflare/workers-types ScheduledController for cron type.

workers/dashboard.description: Next.js 16 dashboard deployed to Cloudflare Workers via OpenNext. Connects to d1-worker and agent-worker via service bindings. Provides real-time trading UI with Framer Motion animations.
workers/dashboard.llmContext: User-facing Next.js 16 dashboard. Deployed to Cloudflare Workers via @opennextjs/cloudflare adapter (NOT Pages, NOT @cloudflare/next-on-pages). Routes: / (main dashboard page), /dashboard/* (auth-protected dashboard pages), /api/* (API routes), /auth/* (login/register). Server Components by default, 'use client' for Framer Motion animations. Connects to d1-worker (D1_SERVICE binding) for data and agent-worker (AGENT_SERVICE binding) for status. Dark mode with shadcn/ui components and Tailwind CSS. OpenNext adapter: 'bunx opennextjs-cloudflare build' → .open-next/worker.js, deployed via 'bunx wrangler deploy'. Static assets served from .open-next/assets/ via ASSETS binding. Next.js ISR cache in NEXT_INC_CACHE_KV. Uses middleware.ts for auth. Layout: app/layout.tsx with globals.css. Error boundary: app/global-error.tsx. Custom 404: app/not-found.tsx. Footer: app/footer.tsx. CI: 'bun run build' checks typecheck + lint + build.

workers/pine-worker.description: Pine Script runtime worker — parses/compiles Pine strategies and runs them at the edge for signal generation.
workers/pine-worker.llmContext: Pine Script worker for strategy parsing and signal generation. Lazy-loads parser bundles for cold-start performance.

d1:trade-data-db.label: trade-data-db (D1)
d1:trade-data-db.description: Cloudflare D1 SQLite database. Stores trade_signals, trades, positions, balances, system_logs tables. Database ID: a682f084-594e-4bd8-be2d-40ea5f8cf42e.
d1:trade-data-db.llmContext: Central SQLite database (D1). Tables: trade_signals (incoming signals with raw payload + parsed status), trades (executed orders with exchange/symbol/quantity/price/status), positions (open/closed positions with unrealized_pnl), balances (exchange/asset balance snapshots with timestamps), system_logs (structured app logs with level/source/message/details), trade_requests, trade_responses (request/response pair audit log). Accessed exclusively through d1-worker service binding (workers/d1-worker). Direct DB binding also available for workers/trade-worker (legacy, for DbLogger) and workers/agent-worker (legacy direct D1 access, being migrated to d1-worker). DB ID: a682f084-594e-4bd8-be2d-40ea5f8cf42e. All queries validated against table allowlist.

r2:trade-reports.label: trade-reports (R2)
r2:trade-reports.description: R2 bucket for storing generated PDF trade reports. Written by trade-worker (post-trade) and report-worker (cron), read by dashboard for download links.
r2:trade-reports.llmContext: R2 bucket for PDF trade reports. Written by trade-worker (saveReportToR2 — per-trade report on successful execution via ctx.waitUntil) and report-worker (generateAndStoreReport — twice-daily portfolio summaries, keys: reports/daily-{timestamp}.pdf). Read by dashboard for user download links. Binding name: REPORTS_BUCKET. Content type: application/pdf.

r2:hoox-system-logs.label: hoox-system-logs (R2)
r2:hoox-system-logs.description: R2 bucket for system log archival storage.
r2:hoox-system-logs.llmContext: R2 bucket for system log archival. Written by trade-worker for operational log storage separate from D1 system_logs table.

r2:user-uploads.label: user-uploads (R2)
r2:user-uploads.description: R2 bucket for user-uploaded files (profile images, attachments, shared media). Used by telegram-worker.
r2:user-uploads.llmContext: R2 bucket for user file storage. Used by telegram-worker for profile images, attachments, and shared media. Accessed via handleGetLatestTradeSignalR2 export.

kv:CONFIG_KV.label: CONFIG_KV
kv:CONFIG_KV.description: KV namespace for configuration storage. Used by 7 workers for feature flags, kill switch, exchange config, secrets, and runtime settings.
kv:CONFIG_KV.llmContext: Shared configuration KV — the most widely used binding (7 workers). Key namespaces: kill_switch (global trading disable), webhook:tradingview:ip_check_enabled and webhook:tradingview:allowed_ips (IP validation), trade:watermark:* (trailing stop tracking by agent-worker), global:* (general settings via d1-worker api/settings), agent:* (agent config overrides), email:* (coin pattern, action pattern, quantity multiplier for email-worker signal parsing). Session rate limiting state (hoox createRateLimiter uses CONFIG_KV). KVKeys exported from @jango-blockchained/hoox-shared/kvKeys provides typed key constants. Used by: hoox, d1-worker, trade-worker, agent-worker, telegram-worker, email-worker, dashboard.

kv:SESSIONS_KV.label: SESSIONS_KV
kv:SESSIONS_KV.description: KV namespace for httpOnly cookie session state. Used exclusively by hoox gateway with 24-hour TTL.
kv:SESSIONS_KV.llmContext: Session state KV for hoox gateway. Stores httpOnly cookie sessions with 24-hour TTL (expirationTtl set on write). Session data includes API key binding, rate limit state, and auth metadata. Accessed exclusively by hoox via sessionManager.

kv:NEXT_INC_CACHE_KV.label: NEXT_INC_CACHE_KV
kv:NEXT_INC_CACHE_KV.description: KV namespace for Next.js Incremental Static Regeneration cache. Used exclusively by dashboard (OpenNext adapter).
kv:NEXT_INC_CACHE_KV.llmContext: Next.js ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration) cache for dashboard. Managed automatically by @opennextjs/cloudflare adapter. Enables hybrid static/dynamic rendering for dashboard pages. Not accessed manually.

queue:trade-execution.label: trade-execution (Queue)
queue:trade-execution.description: Cloudflare Queue for async trade execution. Producer: hoox (POST /webhook). Consumer: trade-worker (queue handler). Provides backpressure, retry with exponential backoff (max 5, delays: 0s/30s/1m/5m/15m), and dead-letter queue on exhaustion.
queue:trade-execution.llmContext: Trade execution queue — the primary async processing pipeline. hoox produces TradeQueueMessage (requestId, exchange, action, symbol, quantity, price, leverage) via sendTradeToQueue. trade-worker consumes in queue() handler with retry logic: max 5 attempts with exponential backoff [0s, 30s, 1m, 5m, 15m], msg.retry({delaySeconds}), automatic DLQ after exhaustion. Failed trades logged to D1 system_logs table and notification sent via telegram-worker. Provides backpressure handling for burst signal scenarios.

do:IDEMPOTENCY_STORE.label: IdempotencyStore (Durable Object)
do:IDEMPOTENCY_STORE.description: Durable Object for signal idempotency. Ensures each trading signal is processed exactly once — crucial for webhook reliability with TradingView retries.
do:IDEMPOTENCY_STORE.llmContext: Idempotency Durable Object in hoox gateway. Prevents duplicate trade processing when TradingView retries webhook delivery. Each signal identified by idempotency key (derived from signal content), stored in DO durable storage. checkIdempotency() returns true if key already processed. DO class exported from workers/hoox/src/index.ts as export { IdempotencyStore }. Single DO instance with consistent routing based on idempotency key. Critical for webhook reliability — without this, retried webhooks would execute duplicate trades.

ai:WORKERS_AI.label: Workers AI
ai:WORKERS_AI.description: Cloudflare Workers AI binding. Used by 3 workers: hoox (signal content validation), agent-worker (risk assessment, multi-provider routing), telegram-worker (message summarization).
ai:WORKERS_AI.llmContext: Workers AI inference binding — primary AI provider in the multi-provider chain (workers-ai → openai → anthropic → google). Used by: hoox (signal content validation, lightweight inference), agent-worker (risk assessment as primary — ProviderManager defaults to workers-ai with model map for routing, also supports embeddings via runEmbedding), telegram-worker (message summarization via Workers AI before sending Telegram notifications). Also accessible via agent-worker's POST /agent/test-model and POST /agent/chat endpoints.

vectorize:my-rag-index.label: my-rag-index (Vectorize)
vectorize:my-rag-index.description: Vectorize index for Retrieval-Augmented Generation. Used by hoox (signal context matching) and telegram-worker (semantic search for notification context).
vectorize:my-rag-index.llmContext: Vectorize vector search index for RAG pipeline. Used by hoox for signal context matching (find similar historical signals for validation) and telegram-worker for semantic search (find relevant context before generating notification summaries). Embeddings generated via Workers AI (runEmbedding in agent-worker, generateEmbeddings in telegram-worker). Operations: queryEmbeddings (semantic search by vector similarity), insertEmbeddings (store new embeddings), generateEmbeddings (create embeddings from text). Index name: my-rag-index.

analytics:hoox-analytics.label: hoox-analytics (Analytics Engine)
analytics:hoox-analytics.description: Cloudflare Analytics Engine dataset. Receives time-series events exclusively from analytics-worker. Queryable via SQL API for dashboard and reporting.
analytics:hoox-analytics.llmContext: Analytics Engine dataset for time-series metric storage. Single writer: analytics-worker (writeDataPoint writes blobs + doubles + indexes per event type). Event types: api-call (worker, endpoint, latencyMs, success — most frequent), trade (exchange, symbol, action, quantity, result), worker-perf (worker, requests, errors, duration), signal (source, type, symbol, confidence), notification (type, target, success). Queryable via Cloudflare SQL API (REST POST with bearer token, requires CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN + CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID). SQL queries built via buildQuery helper: getTradeMetrics, getTradesByExchange, getTradeSuccessRate, getWorkerPerformance, getApiCallStats, getSignalOutcomes.

browser:REPORT_RENDERER.label: Browser Rendering
browser:REPORT_RENDERER.description: Cloudflare Browser Rendering REST API. Used exclusively by report-worker to convert HTML reports to A4 PDFs with printBackground and custom margins.
browser:REPORT_RENDERER.llmContext: Browser Rendering REST API for serverless PDF generation. Used exclusively by report-worker's generatePdf() function. API: POST https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/{account}/browser-rendering/pdf, Bearer token auth (CF_API_TOKEN_BINDING). Request body: { html: string, options: { format: 'A4', printBackground: true, margin: { top/bottom/left/right: '1cm' } } }. Not accessed via service binding — called as external REST API. Returns ArrayBuffer PDF. Requires account-level API token with Browser Rendering permissions. Triggered twice daily via cron (06:00/18:00 UTC) or on-demand via GET /report endpoint.

binding:TELEGRAM_SERVICE.label: TELEGRAM_SERVICE (Service Binding)
binding:TELEGRAM_SERVICE.description: Cloudflare Service Binding to telegram-worker. Used by 5 workers to send notifications, alerts, and report delivery via Telegram Bot API.
binding:TELEGRAM_SERVICE.llmContext: Service binding to telegram-worker for sending notifications. Target endpoint: POST /webhook (primary) or POST /process (legacy). Called by: hoox (kill switch alerts, signal confirmation), trade-worker (trade execution notifications), agent-worker (risk alerts, trailing stop triggers), report-worker (PDF report delivery with signed URL), web3-wallet-worker (wallet initialization confirmation). Fire-and-forget via ctx.waitUntil. NotificationPayload: { message: string, chatId?: string } — uses TG_CHAT_ID_BINDING if chatId omitted.

binding:TRADE_SERVICE.label: TRADE_SERVICE (Service Binding)
binding:TRADE_SERVICE.description: Cloudflare Service Binding to trade-worker. Used by 3 workers to forward trading signals for execution.
binding:TRADE_SERVICE.llmContext: Service binding to trade-worker for trade signal execution. Target endpoint: POST /webhook. Called by: hoox (queue producer fallback), email-worker (parsed email signals with X-Internal-Auth-Key + X-Source: email-worker headers), agent-worker (AI-initiated risk adjustments). Trade execution via exchange router (Binance/MEXC/Bybit) with exponential backoff retry.

binding:D1_SERVICE.label: D1_SERVICE (Service Binding)
binding:D1_SERVICE.description: Cloudflare Service Binding to d1-worker. Used by 4 workers for centralized database operations.
binding:D1_SERVICE.llmContext: Service binding to d1-worker for centralized D1 database access. Provides table-allowlist-validated SQL execution (allowed tables: trade_signals, trades, positions, balances, system_logs, trade_requests, trade_responses). Endpoints: POST /query (single SQL), POST /batch (atomic multi-statement), GET/POST /api/settings, GET /api/balances, GET /api/positions, GET /api/logs. All endpoints except /health require internal auth. Called by: trade-worker (trade persistence, balance tracking), report-worker (portfolio summary for PDF reports), dashboard (data read for UI rendering), agent-worker (position monitoring for AI risk assessment).

binding:AGENT_SERVICE.label: AGENT_SERVICE (Service Binding)
binding:AGENT_SERVICE.description: Cloudflare Service Binding to agent-worker. Used by dashboard for AI risk management status and on-demand housekeeping triggers.
binding:AGENT_SERVICE.llmContext: Service binding to agent-worker for AI risk management. Target endpoints: POST /agent/housekeeping (trigger on-demand housekeeping), GET /agent/status (provider config + active trailing stops). Called by: dashboard (status page display, manual housekeeping trigger). Returns AI risk assessment results including open positions, trailing stop status, and risk threshold compliance.

dataFlows.description: Trading signal: POST /webhook → validate webhook + check kill_switch + check IP allowlist + deduplicate via IdempotencyStore DO + check rate limit → produce TradeQueueMessage to TRADE_QUEUE → trade-worker consumes from queue handler with exponential backoff retry (max 5)

Trade data persistence: executeTrade → on success, write trade record + update positions + log to system_logs via d1-worker POST /query (INSERT/UPDATE) with X-Internal-Auth-Key. Reported via DbLogger request/response log pairs.

Trade notification: executeTrade → on success or failure, call telegram-worker POST /process (legacy) or POST /webhook with NotificationPayload { message, chatId? } via TELEGRAM_SERVICE service binding

Trade analytics: executeTrade → trackAnalytics(env, '/track/api-call', { worker: 'trade-worker', endpoint, latencyMs, success }) for every /webhook and /process call. Fire-and-forget via ctx.waitUntil.

Position monitoring: runHousekeeping → reads open positions from D1 via D1_SERVICE binding (POST /query: SELECT * FROM positions WHERE status = 'OPEN'). Checks trailing stops from CONFIG_KV (prefix: trade:watermark:).
Risk adjustment: processRoutine → AI risk assessment → if adjustment needed, calls trade-worker POST /webhook via TRADE_SERVICE binding with X-Internal-Auth-Key. Close orders via sendCloseOrder.
Risk alert: runHousekeeping/processRoutine → on risk threshold breach or trailing stop triggered, calls telegram-worker POST /webhook with alert message via TELEGRAM_SERVICE binding. Fire-and-forget.

Email signal: Mailgun webhook POST /webhook → HMAC-SHA256 signature validation → parseEmailSignal (JSON or plaintext regex extraction) → forward parsed EmailSignal to trade-worker POST /webhook via TRADE_SERVICE binding with X-Internal-Auth-Key header
Email analytics: on signal forward (success or failure), trackAnalytics(env, '/track/signal', { source: 'email-worker', type, symbol, confidence }). Fire-and-forget via ctx.waitUntil.
DB analytics: on every POST /query and POST /batch execution, trackAnalytics(env, '/track/api-call', { worker: 'd1-worker', endpoint, latencyMs, success }). Fire-and-forget via ctx.waitUntil.
Notification analytics: after each notification send, trackAnalytics via internal function call (not service binding). Tracks notification type, target, and success status.
Web3 notification: on wallet initialization, calls telegram-worker POST /webhook with wallet address notification via TELEGRAM_SERVICE service binding. Fire-and-forget via ctx.waitUntil.
Web3 analytics: after wallet init, trackAnalytics(env, '/track/api-call', { worker: 'web3-wallet-worker', endpoint: '/', latencyMs, success }). Fire-and-forget via ctx.waitUntil.

Report delivery: generateAndStoreReport → on PDF stored in R2, calls telegram-worker POST /process via TELEGRAM_SERVICE binding with markdown message including signed report URL. Fire-and-forget via ctx.waitUntil.

Dashboard data read: Next.js server components call d1-worker service binding (GET /api/balances, GET /api/positions, GET /api/settings, GET /api/logs, POST /query) for dashboard page rendering and API routes. Passes X-Internal-Auth-Key via env.D1_SERVICE binding.
Dashboard status: dashboard calls agent-worker POST /agent/housekeeping via AGENT_SERVICE binding to trigger on-demand AI risk assessment and display results on dashboard status page.

communities.label: Cloudflare Workers
communities.description: All 10 worker services in the Hoox trading system

communities.label: Cloudflare Infrastructure
communities.description: D1, R2, KV, Queue, DO, AI, Vectorize, Analytics Engine, Browser Rendering

communities.label: Signal Processing Pipeline
communities.description: The core trading signal flow: hoox receives webhooks → deduplicate via IdempotencyStore DO → produce to TRADE_QUEUE → trade-worker consumes with retry → execute on Binance/MEXC/Bybit → persist to D1 → notify via telegram → track via analytics-worker. Includes infrastructure nodes that enable this flow (queue, DO, D1 database).

communities.label: AI & Risk Management
communities.description: Autonomous AI risk management subsystem: agent-worker (5-min cron) reads positions from D1, assesses risk via Workers AI (with multi-provider fallback: workers-ai → openai → anthropic → google), adjusts positions via trade-worker, sends alerts via telegram-worker. Uses Vectorize index for RAG context retrieval. Embeddings generated via Workers AI.
